669→def test_pr_list_displays_enrichment_warnings() -> None: 670→ """Test that enrichment warnings from PlanListData are displayed to the user.""" 691→ warnings=( 692→ "GraphQL enrichment failed for 2/5 PRs " 703→ assert "Warning:" in result.output 704→ assert "GraphQL enrichment failed for 2/5 PRs" in result.output 707→def test_pr_list_no_warnings_when_enrichment_succeeds() -> None: 708→ """Test that no warnings are displayed when enrichment succeeds fully.""" 735→ assert "Warning:" not in result.output

Perfect! I've successfully made all four replacements in the test file `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_list.py`:
## Summary of Changes
1. **`"Found 2 plan(s)"` → `"Found 2 PR(s)"`** - Updated 4 occurrences (lines 76, 219, 354, 454, 585) 2. **`"Found 1 plan(s)"` → `"Found 1 PR(s)"`** - Updated 3 occurrences (lines 124, 178, 664) 3. **`"No plans found matching the criteria"` → `"No PRs found matching the criteria"`** - Updated 1 occurrence (line 250) 4. **`["pr", "list", "--sort", "plan"]` → `["pr", "list", "--sort", "pr"]`** - Updated 1 occurrence (line 351)
All assertion strings have been updated to use "PR" terminology instead of "plan" to match the command's updated output messages.
